hero.subtitle: Automatic link scanning across every page. 16 threat intelligence sources. Beats Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 for Families by 38 percentage points on fresh URLhaus + PhishTank URLs. ML-powered detection. Your browsing data never leaves your device.
hero.cta_footer: Free forever for basic protection. No credit card required.

final_cta.title: Ready to browse safely?
final_cta.subtitle: Join thousands of users who browse without worry.

features.heading: Everything you need. Nothing you don't.

items.title: Automatic Link Scanning
items.desc: Every link on every page checked against 16 threat databases. Red, yellow, green badges show safety at a glance.

items.title: Privacy Audit
items.desc: Right-click any page to see trackers, cookies, data collection, and fingerprinting. Grade A through F.

items.title: ML-Powered Detection
items.desc: CatBoost model trained on 14,400 verified domains. 0.9983 AUC. Catches novel phishing that rule-based systems miss.

items.title: Instant Protection
items.desc: Most domains check instantly against an on-device safe-list. We only contact our servers for unknown domains, and we send only the domain name — never the full URL.

items.title: Your Data, Your Device
items.desc: Browsing history never touches our servers. We only see domain names. Even if breached, your data is safe.

items.title: Inbox Scanner
items.desc: Finds phishing links in your Gmail and Outlook that your browser missed.

steps.title: Install
steps.desc: Add the Chrome extension. Takes 10 seconds.

steps.title: Browse
steps.desc: Cleanway checks every link automatically. No action needed.

steps.title: Stay Safe
steps.desc: Dangerous links get red badges. Click for details.

pricing_teaser.heading: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing_teaser.trial_note: All plans include 14-day free trial. Cancel anytime.

privacy.heading: Privacy is not a feature. It's the architecture.
privacy.server_note: If breached: attacker gets emails + subscription. Boring.
privacy.device_note: If device is lost: protected by OS encryption.

items.q: Finally, a security tool that doesn't spy on me. The Privacy Audit is eye-opening.
Caught 3 phishing links in my Gmail that Chrome missed. Worth every penny.
We replaced KnowBe4 with Cleanway Business. Same protection, 1/4 the price.

items.a: Your browsing history never leaves your device. Our servers store only your email and subscription status. Even if we're breached, attackers learn nothing about your online activity.
No. Most domains resolve instantly against an on-device safe-list. Only unknown domains are sent to our API, and we send only the domain name — never the full URL.

items.q: Can I use it with a VPN?
items.a: Yes! On mobile, Cleanway auto-detects your VPN and switches to DNS mode, working alongside NordVPN, ExpressVPN, or any other provider.

items.q: What's different from Google Safe Browsing?
items.a: Google Safe Browsing is reactive — it catches known threats but misses new phishing. Cleanway adds ML detection, 8 additional threat sources, Privacy Audit, and doesn't send your browsing data to Google.
Yes! The free plan includes 10 API checks/day for unknown domains, unlimited instant checks against the on-device safe-list, and basic Privacy Audit. Most casual users never need to upgrade.
Business plan includes simulated phishing emails sent to your team. Pick a template, we send test emails, track who clicks vs. who reports. For training, not punishment.

dns.page_title: Phishing-blocking DNS — one tap, no app
dns.page_description: Install Cleanway's phishing-blocking DNS resolver in 30 seconds on iPhone, Android, Mac or Windows. System-wide protection without a VPN or an app.
dns.hero_title: Block phishing on every app — without installing one
dns.hero_subtitle: Cleanway runs a phishing-blocking DNS resolver. Point your phone or laptop at it and every app on the device — Safari, Chrome, Mail, banking apps — gets the same protection.
dns.hero_meta: Works on iOS 14+, iPadOS 14+, macOS 11+, Android 9+, Windows 11. No VPN. No battery hit.
dns.ios_intro: Tap the button below on your iPhone or iPad. iOS will ask permission to install a configuration profile.
dns.ios_step1: Tap “Allow” when iOS asks to download the profile.
dns.ios_step2: Open Settings → General → VPN & Device Management → Cleanway DNS.
dns.ios_step3: Tap “Install” twice, then enter your device passcode.
dns.ios_unsigned_note: If iOS shows “Unsigned”, that’s expected — Apple’s signing program is paid and we’d rather not pass the cost on to you. The profile itself is unchanged whether it’s signed or not.
dns.android_intro: Android 9 (Pie) and later have built-in DNS-over-TLS — no app required.
dns.android_step1: Open Settings → Network & internet → Private DNS.
dns.android_compat_note: Some Samsung and Xiaomi devices nest the option under Connections → More connection settings. Search “Private DNS” in Settings if you can’t find it.
dns.win_intro: Windows 11 supports DNS-over-HTTPS natively. Earlier Windows versions need a third-party tool like Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 — sorry.
dns.win_step1: Open Settings → Network & internet → Wi-Fi → click your network → DNS server assignment → Edit.
dns.win_step2: Switch to Manual, turn on IPv4, set preferred DNS to 1.1.1.1 with DNS over HTTPS “On (manual template)”.
dns.benefits_heading: What does this protect me from?
dns.benefit_1: Phishing links in SMS, email, and Telegram open as a “site not found” instead of a credential-stealing page.
dns.benefit_2: Malware command-and-control hosts can’t reach your device — even if a sketchy app you installed tries to phone home.
dns.benefit_3: Brand-impersonation domains (paypal-secure-login.com) are blocked at the resolver level — your browser never even loads them.
dns.benefit_4: Works on every app, not just your browser. WhatsApp links, banking apps, in-app browsers — all protected.
dns.privacy_heading: Privacy — what we see and what we don’t
dns.privacy_body: Cleanway DNS resolves the domain only. We never log the queries to disk, never send them to Sentry, never sell them to anyone. Queries stay in memory just long enough to answer the request.
dns.privacy_logs: What we DO keep: an anonymous daily counter of total queries and total blocks, used to publish our quarterly transparency report. No domains, no IPs, no users.

methodology.page_description: Cleanway's per-domain detection results vs Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 for Families, Google Safe Browsing, PhishTank, and VirusTotal — measured weekly on fresh URLhaus + PhishTank URLs. Reproducible benchmark script linked.
methodology.hero_title: How we measure our own detection rate
methodology.hero_subtitle: Every recall number we publish points here. The dataset, the script, and a button to clone it on GitHub.
methodology.why_p1: Every other anti-phishing vendor publishes glossy quarterly threat reports without their false-positive rate or per-vendor recall comparison. The numbers exist only in their internal dashboards.
methodology.why_p2: We do the opposite: this page runs the same Python script anyone can clone, every Monday, against fresh URLhaus + PhishTank phishing URLs, and publishes the raw results. The script's output IS the marketing claim.
methodology.results_unavailable: Latest benchmark snapshot not available — re-running cron now.
methodology.results_meta: Snapshot: $DATE$. Sample: $PHISHING$ phishing URLs, $LEGIT$ Tranco legit URLs.
methodology.safe_table_heading: Legit batch (FPR — false-positive rate, expected verdict: safe)
methodology.verdict_mapping_heading: How we map each resolver's response
methodology.verdict_mapping_p1: Different vendors return different verdict shapes. We normalise to three: dangerous / safe / unknown. Unknown verdicts (rate-limited, not indexed, error) are reported separately — not counted as right or wrong.
methodology.reproduce_p1: Two commands and a Python 3 install. The script fetches the same feeds, runs the same comparisons, writes the same JSON output:
methodology.cadence_p1: The benchmark runs every Monday at 05:15 UTC via a GitHub Actions cron. Results commit to docs/benchmarks/ in this repo. A regression-detection step opens a PR if recall drops more than 5 percentage points week-over-week.
methodology.caveat_1: Cleanway uses the public /api/v1/check endpoint — same anonymous surface a regular user would see. We deliberately do NOT use a privileged path for benchmarking ourselves.
methodology.caveat_2: PhishTank's free check API returns 'not indexed' for most fresh URLhaus URLs because their database lags by a few hours — that's why PhishTank's Recall column often shows '—' (unknown), not because it's broken.
methodology.caveat_3: Google Safe Browsing requires an API key (GOOGLE_SAFE_BROWSING_KEY). If unset, that column shows '—' and is excluded from the comparison.
methodology.caveat_4: Latency p50 measured from this script's host (US/EU CI runner); your latency from Asia or LATAM will differ. The relative comparison stays the same.

preheader.text: Change your password on affected sites soon.

title.text: Your email appeared in a new breach

body_p1.text: Your email was found in a recently disclosed data breach. This means your password on the affected site may be exposed.

body_p2_recommendation.text: Change your password on that site now. If you use the same password elsewhere, change it there too. Consider a password manager — it's the only real fix.

title.text: We'll be here if you come back

body_p1.text: Your subscription has been canceled. Blocking scam sites continues to work for free — this is our ethical invariant, it won't change.

body_p2_keep_free.text: You'll keep your free-tier features: link scanning, block pages, email leak checks, and basic Privacy Audit grades.

subject.text: $NAME$ set up Cleanway protection for you

preheader.text: Simple protection from scams — set up by your family.

title.text: Safety from online scams — set up for you

body_p1_to_grandchild.text: $NAME$ set up Cleanway protection on your devices with extra-large text and voice alerts. You don't need to do anything — it's already working.

body_p2_what_it_does.text: When a scammer tries to trick you with a fake bank website or delivery notice, Cleanway stops it before you enter anything. A clear warning shows in big letters: 'STOP — this is a scam site'.

reassurance.text: If you're not sure what to do, tap the 'Ask family' button in the app — $NAME$ gets a message and can help.
